title: Hideaki Anno NHK Documentary
wikitext:
[]: Re: Hideaki Anno NHK Documentary             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 29, 2021 7:47 am Not yet. Anno Cinema is holding a GoFundMe to do the job.

[]: Re: Hideaki Anno NHK Documentary             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Mon Mar 29, 2021 7:58 am The $590 dollar goal has already been reached, but for whatever reason, that fact has not been announced.

[]: Re: Hideaki Anno NHK Documentary             Quote Post by FazerGS » Sun Apr 04, 2021 9:57 pm I'm new to the forums, so forgive me if this is against the rules, but someone on 4chan translated the NHK Anno Documentary. You can find it here: https://mega.nz/file/Jf5ghJza#1kjp5r5vI ... M6sVuJaHOg from this thread: https://boards.4channel.org/a/thread/219625236 Everything seems legit, but I don't speak Japanese so you might want to verify the subs.

[]: Re: Hideaki Anno NHK Documentary             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Apr 04, 2021 11:35 pm His desktop is art drawn by his wife! That's so cute! That's so # goals. I wish I had a wife who drew art. Or a husband who put my art on his desktop. Anno didn't find her until he was pretty old already so maybe there's still hope for me also the narration style of the documentary is fantastically hilarious

[]: Re: Hideaki Anno NHK Documentary             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Apr 05, 2021 4:05 am Be warned that there are some details of Shin that necessarily leak out, even if they are simple contextless mo-cap renders, or the scenes themselves being captured.

[]: Re: Hideaki Anno NHK Documentary             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 05, 2021 7:54 am A detail I really appreciate is that one of the reasons for the drastic rewrite was apparently wanting to avoid preachyness after one test viewer said it possibly came off that way. though i would want to make at least some criticism about the glorification of workaholicism since that's a systemic problem in Japan.

[]: Re: Hideaki Anno NHK Documentary             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Mon Apr 05, 2021 7:59 am There do seem to be times when 4chan actually manages to provide something of value, huh. I don't think I have time to watch the full doc right now, but even the first minutes seem to hint at something interesting.

[]: Re: Hideaki Anno NHK Documentary             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 05, 2021 8:04 am That bit where Moyoco describes him makes him sound rather Rei-like, all work, no self-care, little self-preservation, etc. She's like Shinji cleaning up the room or bringing the bentos. XDD Though Rei probably wouldn't try to get out of it by acting cutesy lol. & pushing ppl towards their happiness or likening them to cute animals is probably more of a Mari or Misato-like characteristic. There's even a photo of gloomy looking toddler Anno with a striped shirt just like baby Shinji in that one flashback
